insight: Algeria's hydration breaks in the 2026 World Cup have not provided the team with a significant boost, as they failed to gain momentum in both of their breaks, with a mean change of -0.100 minutes per minute, indicating a slight decline in performance. This decline is consistent whether Algeria was pressing before the break or not, suggesting that their strategy going into the break did not have a notable impact on their ability to regain momentum. In the one instance where Algeria was pressing before the break, they still saw a decline in performance, with an average change of -0.100 minutes per minute, and were unable to keep their momentum going after the break. Overall, Algeria's hydration breaks have not been a turning point in their matches, and the team may need to adjust their strategy to capitalize on these breaks and regain their footing.
